instruction:
Setting $A=\left( \begin{matrix} {{{1}}} & {{{1}}} & {{{a}}} \\ {{{1}}} & {{{a}}} & {{{1}}} \\ {{{a}}} & {{{1}}} & {{{1}}} \\ \end{matrix} \right), \beta=\left( \begin{matrix} {{{1}}} \\ {{{1}}} \\ {{{-2}}} \\ \end{matrix} \right)$ . It is known that the linear equation system $AX = \beta$ has infinitely many solutions. Can the value of $a$ be determined?

Options:
A. $$
a=-4
$$
B. $$
a=0
$$
C. $$
a=4
$$
D. $$
a=-1
$$
E. $$
a=-5
$$
F. $$
a=2
$$
G. $$
a=1
$$
H. $$
a=3
$$
I. $$
a=-3
$$
J. $$
a=-2
$$
